claim: Local models are viable for agentic coding when hardware permits (8GB+ VRAM on GPU or 16GB+ unified memory on Mac); you eliminate API costs and gain offline capability
actionable: Evaluate your hardware (GPU VRAM or unified memory); if sufficient, deploy local models instead of paying per-token for cloud APIs
source_title: The Best LOCAL Agentic Coding Workflow

claim: GPU memory (VRAM) on Windows or unified memory on M-series Macs determines max model size; M-series Macs can run larger models (75-80% of RAM usable) but at slower throughput than high-end GPUs like RTX 4090
actionable: On Windows, match model size to GPU VRAM exactly (8GB GPU = 7B model); on Mac, use up to 75% of unified memory but expect slower tokens/sec than dedicated GPUs
source_title: The Best LOCAL Agentic Coding Workflow

claim: Memory bandwidth matters: RTX 4090 has 1,080 GB/sec vs M4 Max 546 GB/sec; higher bandwidth means faster token generation even if model size is comparable
actionable: When comparing hardware, check memory bandwidth specs; a smaller model on high-bandwidth GPU beats a larger model on Mac for real-world latency
source_title: The Best LOCAL Agentic Coding Workflow

claim: For agentic coding, local models must support tool use (not just chat); models without tool-calling cannot edit files or execute bash commands, limiting them to text-only responses
actionable: Verify tool use capability before downloading; Gwen/Qwen models support it; smaller models like Gemma 4 may not; check model cards on HuggingFace
source_title: The Best LOCAL Agentic Coding Workflow

claim: Quantization (Q4, Q3, Q2) shrinks model size and speeds up execution; always use the lowest quantization that fits your VRAM (e.g., Q4 35B instead of full 35B) for speed and capacity
actionable: When downloading models, prefer Q4/Q3 quantized versions; avoid full-precision unless VRAM is abundant; test quantization impact on accuracy for your use case
source_title: The Best LOCAL Agentic Coding Workflow

claim: A3B/A6B/A9B in model names means 'active' parameters; these models achieve better performance on limited hardware than their full parameter count suggests
actionable: Prefer A3B/A6B variants when available; they allow running larger models on smaller VRAM budgets without proportional quality loss
source_title: The Best LOCAL Agentic Coding Workflow

claim: Use two models: small autocomplete model (Gwen 2.5 1.5B) for inline completion, large capable model (Gwen 3.6 35B or Gwen 3 Coder Next) for chat/edit tasks
actionable: Set up dual-model configuration in LM Studio and VS Code; run both models simultaneously; use context length settings to balance VRAM usage and capability
source_title: The Best LOCAL Agentic Coding Workflow

claim: LM Studio exposes a local API endpoint (http://localhost:1234/v1) that VS Code, Continue extension, and other IDEs can connect to; this decouples model serving from editing
actionable: Run LM Studio as a background server; configure VS Code's language models settings to point to http://localhost:1234/v1; test the connection before using in chat
source_title: The Best LOCAL Agentic Coding Workflow

claim: VS Code's native language models feature (vs Continue extension) provides better file editing/apply integration; both support custom endpoint configuration via settings JSON
actionable: Prefer VS Code native chat for editing; use Continue for autocomplete configuration; keep both active for full local agentic workflow
source_title: The Best LOCAL Agentic Coding Workflow

claim: Gwen family models (Qwen) are best-in-class for local agentic coding due to tool use support, reasonable sizes, and strong performance; Gemma 4 is newer but weaker at coding tasks
actionable: Default to Gwen 2.5/3.5/3.6/3 Coder Next depending on VRAM; avoid Gemma 4 if coding is primary use case
source_title: The Best LOCAL Agentic Coding Workflow

claim: Local models achieve 50-100 tokens/second (fast enough for chat) but are slower than cloud APIs; useful for iteration/exploration, not replacement for Opus for complex tasks
actionable: Use local models for small edits, exploration, and refactoring; keep cloud API access for complex architectural decisions or when offline is not needed
source_title: The Best LOCAL Agentic Coding Workflow
